Rhode Island · State Licensing Reference

Dietitian
Licensed Dietitian/Nutritionist (LDN) Guide

Issued by the Board of Dietetics Practice under the Rhode Island Department of Health. Data verified for 2026.

Visit Official Board Portal
Fees Board Contact License Types How to Apply Documents Reciprocity FAQ
Total Initial Fee
$75
$75 non-refundable application fee
Renewal
$170
Every 2 years (expires Nov 1 of odd years) + 20 CE hours
Processing Time
3 to 5 Weeks
Subject to board volume
License Duration
Up to 2 Years
From date of issuance
Board Contact

Board of Dietetics Practice

Mailing Address

Center for Professional Licensing, Room 104, 3 Capitol Hill
Providence, RI 02908

Physical Address

3 Capitol Hill
Providence, RI 02908

Phone & Fax

Fax: (401) 222-1272

Email

Credential Categories

Rhode Island Dietitian License Types

Licensed Dietitian/Nutritionist (LDN)

The credential to practice and provide medical nutrition therapy in Rhode Island, overseen by the Board of Dietetics Practice.

Application Process

How to Apply for a Rhode Island Licensed Dietitian/Nutritionist (LDN)

Complete Education & Supervised Practice

Earn a master's degree from an ACEND-accredited program. Complete a minimum of 900 hours of board-approved supervised dietetic experience (most accredited programs exceed this with 1,000+ hours).

Pass the CDR Examination

Successfully pass the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) Registration Examination for Dietitians. Verification of current RD status is required.

Submit the Application and Fee

Complete, sign, and notarize the official application. Submit it to the Department of Health with the $75 fee (payable to the RI General Treasurer) and a 2x3 portrait photograph.

Complete Background Check & Verifications

Obtain an original Bureau of Criminal Identification (BCI) report from the Attorney General's Office. Ensure CDR verification is sent directly to the Board.

Required Documents

Application Document Checklist

  • Notarized Application & PhotoApplication form with a notarized signature and a recent 2x3 head-and-shoulders photograph.
  • CDR Registration VerificationProof of active registration sent directly from the CDR to the Board.
  • Criminal Background CheckAn original BCI report from the Rhode Island Attorney General's Office.
Practice Laws

Provisional Practice & Reciprocity

Provisional Rules

Rhode Island offers a temporary permit for individuals who have completed their education and supervised practice but are awaiting the results of the CDR exam. A temporary permit holder may practice under supervision for 90 days and use the title 'graduate dietitian/nutritionist'.

Reciprocity & Endorsement

Rhode Island offers licensure by endorsement for dietitians/nutritionists actively licensed in other states, provided the requirements of the other state are equivalent to Rhode Island's. Applying via endorsement requires submitting the application, fee, and verification of all out-of-state licenses.

Frequently Asked Questions

Rhode Island Dietitian FAQ

How much does a Rhode Island dietitian license cost?

The initial cost is a $75 application fee, which does not include the cost of the BCI background check.

When does the Rhode Island license need to be renewed?

Rhode Island LDN licenses expire on November 1st of odd-numbered years. The biennial renewal fee is $170.

Does Rhode Island use the title 'Nutritionist'?

Yes, Rhode Island uses the combined title of Licensed Dietitian/Nutritionist (LDN) to license both professionals under one standard for medical nutrition therapy.
